name: whisper-stock-avoid
trap_lesson: Whisper stocks seduce with a thrilling, world-changing story and little or no earnings to back it. The exciting narrative is the bait. Long shots almost never pay off.
query: A friend whispered that CURE (Helios Biocure) has a revolutionary therapy that could 'change medicine forever' and 10x from here. Research it using the given facts.

given_facts.business: Pre-revenue biotech with a single experimental therapy in early trials; pitched as a world-changing miracle cure.
given_facts.story_claims: single binary trial outcome; 'could cure a major disease'; no product, no revenue

expected_behavior: Identifies CURE as a whisper stock / long shot: a thrilling story with no earnings (and no revenue) to value.
States that with negative EPS and zero revenue, P/E and PEG are not meaningful — there is no fundamentals story to tell, only a binary bet.
Treats the 'change medicine forever' / '10x from here' framing as the bait, exactly the kind of excitement that should raise skepticism.
Notes the cash runway math (cash ~120 vs ~90/yr burn → roughly one year) as a survival risk, not a thesis.
Does not attempt a two-minute growth story it cannot support; concludes a real Lynch story cannot be constructed.
Verdict is Avoid/Pass, justified by absence of earnings and reliance on a single binary outcome — not a Buy because the upside sounds huge.
Includes the (dominant) bear case; remains honest that the science is outside the method's competence (know what you own).
Any figures used cite a source + as-of date.
